message:
fix+refactor: correct mutex waker logic, prevent scope deadlock, hoist nested types to module level

Bug fixes:
- sync/mutex.rs: Fix inverted conditional in LockFuture::drop and
  OwnedMutexGuard that suppressed waker propagation when the mutex was
  unlocked. The old code returned None (no wake) when !state.locked was
  true, meaning it skipped waking the next waiter precisely when the
  mutex was available. Swapping the branches ensures the front waiter
  is woken when the lock is free.

- cx/scope.rs: Replace indefinite `h1.join(cx).await` in the backup
  admission failure path with a single best-effort poll using
  Waker::noop(). Without an active scheduler (e.g. in unit tests),
  awaiting join would deadlock because nothing drives the cancelled
  task to completion. A single poll gives the task a chance to resolve
  immediately while avoiding the hang.

- cx/scope.rs (tests): Update child_region_lifecycle and
  child_region_inherits_budget tests to assert that closed child
  regions are reclaimed from the arena (region().is_none()) rather
  than checking for RegionState::Closed. The budget test now captures
  the child budget inside the async block before the region is
  reclaimed.

Refactoring (no behavior change):
- cx/scope.rs: Hoist RegionRunner and RegionCloseFuture from inline
  nested structs inside methods to module-level definitions. Improves
  readability and makes them available for direct unit testing.

- http/h1/client.rs: Extract the HTTP response body-kind determination
  logic into a standalone response_body_kind() function, reducing deep
  nesting in the main response parsing loop.

- runtime/io_driver.rs: Unify two duplicate PollingGuard
  implementations (one that cleared is_polling, one that didn't) into
  a single struct with a clear_poll_flag parameter. Add proper accessor
  methods (events_mut, take_events) replacing raw Option unwraps. Add
  is_polling to the Debug impl. Insert explicit drop(driver) before
  returning wakers so the lock is released before wake calls.

- runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: Extract the I/O polling phase from
  the main worker loop into a drive_io_phase() method. Simplifies the
  central scheduling loop and makes the I/O logic independently
  testable. Also simplifies timeout computation using map() instead of
  if-let.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
